component_layouts.spatial_behavior: No visual footprint; behaves as an invisible logic layer within the page structure.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact form is positioned in the central column directly beneath the introductory text of the pale green panel. There is a generous vertical gap of roughly 32–48px between the bottom of the intro paragraph and the top edge of the form container, maintaining the open editorial feel. The form container spans approximately 70–80% of the page width, centered, leaving wide symmetrical margins on the left and right so fields do not approach the edges. Internally, the form has comfortable padding (around 24px top and bottom, 32px left and right on desktop), and its fields are stacked vertically in a single column to echo the mockup’s linear flow. Groups of related fields (e.g., name and email vs. message body) are separated with slightly larger vertical gaps (24–32px) to visually segment the form without adding borders. The submit button is left-aligned with the input fields and sits with a clear buffer (around 24px) from the last field, so the bottom of the form still feels spacious before transitioning to the content below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level element in the main vertical flow; it expands vertically as needed and pushes subsequent components downward without overlapping them.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact form status banner appears tightly coupled with the contact form within the same central column. When visible, it sits immediately above the form’s first field or at the top of the form container area, with a vertical gap of about 16–24px from the bottom of the introductory paragraph so that users read it as part of the form context, not as part of the hero text. The banner width matches the form’s content width (around 70–80% of the page, centered), and it is styled as a low-height horizontal strip with subtle background color and rounded corners, keeping to the minimalist aesthetic. There is comfortable internal padding (16–24px) so text can wrap across multiple short lines without feeling cramped. When absent, the space collapses and the form shifts upward so the vertical rhythm remains tight and uncluttered.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard in-flow block that appears and disappears without affecting the left/right alignment of nearby components; it simply expands the vertical stack when present.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The alternative contact panel is placed beneath the contact form as the next major block in the central column. A generous gap of about 48–64px separates the bottom of the form (or its submit button area) from the top of this panel, reinforcing that this is secondary information. The panel spans slightly less than the form width to feel like a contained card—roughly 70% of page width, centered within the pale green panel, with wide outer gutters left and right. It appears as a soft, lightly bordered or subtly shaded box with rounded corners and internal padding of roughly 24px top/bottom and 32px left/right. Within that card, headings, icons, and contact details are arranged in a calm vertical stack with 16–24px spacing between lines, maintaining the loose, editorial density. The panel’s height is driven by its content, but it should not feel tall or crowded; instead, it should look like a concise, easily scannable block of alternative options (phone, email, mailing address, etc.).
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard in-flow block; it stays aligned to the center column and moves down as the form grows taller, preserving consistent margins around it.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ link block sits at the bottom of the main contact content, directly below the alternative contact panel. A vertical gap of about 32–48px separates it from the panel above, visually signaling that this is the final, tertiary action. It aligns with the same central content column, spanning roughly the same 70–80% width, and is left-aligned within that space. Visually, it is simple and light: a short line of explanatory text (for example, a sentence pointing users to frequently asked questions) with an inline link styled in the site’s accent color. The text uses the same line length and line height as the intro paragraph at the top, creating a sense of symmetry between the beginning and end of the page. There is modest bottom padding (around 48–64px) below this block before the pale green panel transitions to the page’s footer, preserving the sense of calm, open space.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard in-flow block at the end of the main column; it scrolls away with the rest of the content and does not stick or float.

name: FAQ
title: FAQ
description: Frequently asked questions about using the site’s tools and features, with search, filtering, and direct navigation to related sections or contact options.

core_features: Load all FAQ entries via getFaqEntries and group them by category.
Allow users to filter FAQs by category (e.g., events, donations, carbon calculator, lifestyle quiz, volunteering, general) using URL parameters.
Provide keyword search across FAQ questions and answers, with the search term reflected in the URL.
Support deep-linking to a specific FAQ entry via URL parameters, automatically expanding and scrolling to that entry.
Detect FAQ entries that reference a related_tool_key and render contextual navigation links (e.g., to events.html or carbon_calculator.html) in the answer area.
Display a clear call-to-action for additional help that navigates to contact.html.
Handle loading and error states for getFaqEntries and display a useful empty state when no FAQs match the current filters/search.
user_workflows:
Browse FAQs by category:
1. User opens faq.html without URL parameters.
2. The page calls getFaqEntries.
3. When data loads, the page derives the available categories from faq_entries[].category.
4. The default view selects either the category from the URL (?category=...) or a default state of "all categories" if no category is specified.
5. User clicks a category filter control (e.g., "Events", "Donations").
6. The page updates the URL parameter ?category=selectedCategory (preserving other parameters like q and entryId).
7. The FAQ list filters client-side to show only entries whose category matches the selectedCategory.
8. The user can expand or collapse individual FAQ entries to read their answers.

Search within FAQs:
1. User opens faq.html or is already on the FAQ page.
2. User types keywords into the search input (e.g., "webinar", "donation receipt").
3. On search submit, the page updates the URL parameter ?q=searchTerm (keeping current category and any entryId).
4. The page re-applies client-side filtering to faq_entries, matching question and answer text against the searchTerm.
5. Only FAQ entries matching the search term (and category, if set) are displayed.
6. User can clear the search, which removes the q parameter and shows all entries for the selected category again.

Open a specific FAQ from a deep link (e.g., from Contact page or inline help):
1. User clicks a link from elsewhere on the site that points to faq.html with an entryId parameter (e.g., faq.html?entryId={id}).
2. On load, the page calls getFaqEntries.
3. After data is loaded, the page locates the FAQ entry whose id matches entryId.
4. If the entry is found, the page determines its category and, if no category is set in the URL, sets the category filter to that entry’s category (updating ?category= if needed).
5. The component automatically expands this FAQ entry and scrolls it into view.
6. If entryId does not match any FAQ, the page shows a non-blocking message (e.g., inline note near the top) but still displays FAQs based on the current category/search.

Navigate from FAQs to related tools (events, carbon calculator, etc.) or Contact:
1. User expands an FAQ entry.
2. If the entry has related_tool_key corresponding to a known tool, the entry displays a contextual link (e.g., “Open events tool”, “Go to carbon calculator”) whose href is set according to related_tool_key (e.g., events.html, carbon_calculator.html, or other tool pages defined by the application).
3. User clicks the contextual link.
4. The browser navigates to the appropriate target page (e.g., events.html or carbon_calculator.html), optionally including query parameters as defined by navigation rules (e.g., a pre-selected context or section on the target page).
5. Alternatively, if the user still needs help after browsing FAQs, they click the global “Still need help? Contact us” control.
6. The browser navigates to contact.html (optionally including a source parameter such as contact.html?from=faq so the contact page can pre-fill or tag the inquiry).
interactions:
Category selection:
- Interaction: User clicks a category filter (button, tab, or dropdown option).
- Behavior: The selected category is stored in the in-memory page state and reflected in the URL as ?category=categoryValue.
- Result: The FAQ list re-renders to show only faq_entries where entry.category equals categoryValue (or all entries if the categoryValue represents an "all" state).

Keyword search:
- Interaction: User types into the search input and submits the form (e.g., presses Enter or clicks a search button).
- Behavior: The search term is stored in the page state and reflected in the URL as ?q=searchTerm.
- Result: The FAQ list is filtered client-side to entries whose question or answer text contains the searchTerm (case-insensitive), in addition to any active category filter.

FAQ entry expand/collapse:
- Interaction: User clicks on an FAQ entry header/question.
- Behavior: If the entry is currently collapsed, it expands; if expanded, it collapses.
- Result: The expanded state is maintained in local component state (not in URL); for a deep-linked entryId, the initial state of that entry is expanded.

Deep-link entry handling:
- Interaction: Page loads with ?entryId=someId in the URL.
- Behavior: After getFaqEntries resolves, the page attempts to find faq_entries[i].id === entryId. If found, that entry is marked as the primary expanded entry, and the scroll logic scrolls it into the viewport.
- Result: User is taken directly to the relevant answer; category filter is optionally adjusted so that the entry is visible in the current list.

Contextual tool links in answers:
- Interaction: User sees an expanded FAQ entry whose related_tool_key is set.
- Behavior: The component renders a link whose href is derived from related_tool_key. For example, if related_tool_key indicates the events tool, the href is events.html; if it indicates the carbon calculator, the href is carbon_calculator.html.
- Result: On click, the user navigates to the appropriate tool page using standard link navigation.

"Still need help?" CTA:
- Interaction: User clicks a prominent "Still need help? Contact us" link or button.
- Behavior: The browser navigates to contact.html (optionally including query parameters such as from=faq and possibly carrying the current category/q for context).
- Result: User can submit a contact form on the contact page if FAQs did not resolve their question.

Empty and error states:
- Interaction: After applying filters/search, no FAQs match.
- Behavior: The FAQ list component shows an empty-state message indicating that no FAQs were found for the current category/search, and offers a quick way to clear filters or go to contact.html.
- Error case: If getFaqEntries fails, an error message appears with a retry action that calls getFaqEntries again.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The FAQ page state is controlled solely through URL parameters and in-memory component state; no localStorage is used. The key URL parameters are:
- category: string representing the current FAQ category filter. On initial load, the page reads window.location.search and, if category is present, applies it as the active category. If absent, the page shows an "all categories" view or a default category. When user changes category through the UI, the page updates the URL query string (preserving other parameters like q and entryId) using the History API (pushState/replaceState) and re-applies the filter.
- q: string containing the current search query. On load, if q is present, the search input is pre-filled with q and the FAQ list is filtered accordingly. Submitting a search form updates q in the URL and triggers re-filtering. Clearing the search removes q from the URL and shows all matching entries for the selected category.
- entryId: string identifying a specific FAQ entry for deep-linking. On load, the page defers handling of entryId until after getFaqEntries completes. If a matching FAQ entry is found, that entry is expanded and scrolled into view; if no matching entry exists, entryId is ignored after showing a non-blocking notice. entryId is not changed by normal browsing; it only appears when the user arrived from a deep link or when explicitly generated by other parts of the application.
All filtering and search is performed client-side on the data returned by getFaqEntries, since the interface does not accept filter parameters. The raw faq_entries array is kept as a full dataset, and derived arrays (filtered by category/q) are computed in memory. URL parameter changes do not trigger additional getFaqEntries calls; instead, they control how the retrieved data is presented.

components.functionality: Initializes the FAQ page, orchestrates loading data from getFaqEntries, parses URL parameters (category, q, entryId), manages top-level state (all entries, filtered entries, loading/error flags), and coordinates communication between the search form, category filter, FAQ list, and contact CTA.
event_handlers: onPageLoad: Calls getFaqEntries with no parameters, parses window.location.search to determine initial category, q, and entryId values, and stores them in controller state.

onFaqEntriesLoaded: Receives faq_entries from getFaqEntries, computes available categories (distinct values of faq_entries[].category), then computes the initial filtered list using current category and q state; if entryId is set, instructs faq-entry-list to auto-expand that entry.
onFaqEntriesError: Sets an error flag and passes error state down to faq-list-wrapper and other dependent components.

onCategoryChange(categoryValue): Updates internal state.category, updates URL parameter category (preserving q and entryId), computes a new filtered list, and notifies faq-category-filter and faq-entry-list of the change.
onSearchSubmit(searchTerm): Updates internal state.q, updates URL parameter q (preserving category and entryId), recomputes the filtered FAQ list, and notifies faq-search-form and faq-entry-list.
onSearchClear: Clears internal state.q, removes q from the URL, recomputes filtered entries based on category only.
onRetryLoad: Re-invokes getFaqEntries if the initial call failed, resetting loading/error flags.

onDeepLinkEntryResolved(entryId): After entries load, identifies the matching entry, ensures its category is included in the active filter (updating category if needed), and passes entryId down so that faq-entry-list scrolls and expands it.

components.functionality: Provides a text input and submit control for users to search FAQ questions and answers. Synchronizes its value with the q URL parameter and triggers filtering via the page controller.
data_binding: Controller state.q (derived from URL parameter q)
event_handlers: onMount: Reads the current q value from controller state and sets the input's value so that the search term is preserved across navigation and reloads.
onInputChange(value): Updates internal component state; does not immediately change filtering until submit or optional debounce logic (if implemented by the app).
onSubmit: Prevents default form submission, then calls faq-page-controller.onSearchSubmit with the current input value.
onClearClick: Clears the input field, then calls faq-page-controller.onSearchClear to remove the q parameter and reset filters.

components.functionality: Shows available FAQ categories (derived from faq_entries[].category) as selectable options (tabs, buttons, or list). Controls which category of FAQs is currently displayed and reflects the category URL parameter.
data_binding: Controller state.category (derived from URL parameter category)
event_handlers: onMount: Requests the current category value from the page controller and highlights the corresponding category option. If no category is set, highlights an "all" state.
onCategoryOptionClick(categoryValue): Prevents default navigation (if any), then calls faq-page-controller.onCategoryChange(categoryValue).

onCategoriesUpdate: When the set of available categories changes due to newly loaded faq_entries, updates the options list and ensures that the current category is valid; if the current category is no longer available, notifies the controller to reset to an "all" or default category.

components.functionality: Wraps the FAQ list, handling loading, error, and empty states for the FAQ content area. It decides whether to show a loading indicator, an error message with retry, an empty state message, or the actual FAQ entry list.
data_binding: Controller state.filteredFaqEntries (derived from getFaqEntries.faq_entries, category, and q)
event_handlers: onRetryClick: Calls faq-page-controller.onRetryLoad when the user chooses to retry after an error.

onStateChange: Listens for changes in loading/error/filteredFaqEntries and shows or hides the faq-entry-list accordingly. If filteredFaqEntries is empty and there is no error, it triggers the display of an empty state with optional guidance to adjust filters or contact support.

components.functionality: Displays the currently filtered list of FAQ entries as expandable/collapsible items. Supports auto-expansion and scrolling for a deep-linked entryId. Within each entry, it delegates rendering of contextual tool links and additional help prompts.
data_binding: Controller state.filteredFaqEntries (array of FAQ objects from getFaqEntries)
Controller state.entryId (from URL parameter entryId, if present)
event_handlers: onMount: Receives the current filteredFaqEntries and optional entryId. If entryId is provided, it identifies the matching entry in the list, marks it as expanded, and calls onScrollToEntry for that id.
onEntryHeaderClick(entryId): Toggles the expanded/collapsed state for the given entryId in internal component state. Only affects this component; does not change URL parameters.
onScrollToEntry(entryId): When instructed (typically after deep-link resolution), scrolls the DOM element corresponding to this entry into view and ensures it is expanded.

onFilteredListChange: When the controller updates filteredFaqEntries (due to category or q change), reconciles expansion state so that:
- If entryId is set and exists in the new filtered list, keeps it expanded and visible.
- If currently expanded entries are outside the new list, collapses them.

onRelatedToolClick(entryId, related_tool_key): Receives click events from nested faq-entry-item components and delegates navigation logic (e.g., which URL to open) to a routing utility or directly sets window.location based on related_tool_key.

components.functionality: Represents a single FAQ entry with its question and answer. Provides an interactive header to expand/collapse the answer, and renders contextual links based on related_tool_key (e.g., to events.html, carbon_calculator.html, or other tools).
data_binding: Single FAQ object from filteredFaqEntries (fields: id, question, answer, category, related_tool_key)
event_handlers: onHeaderClick: Triggers faq-entry-list.onEntryHeaderClick with this entry's id to toggle its expanded state.

onRelatedToolLinkRender: Based on faqEntry.related_tool_key, constructs an href to the appropriate page. For example:
  - If related_tool_key corresponds to the events tool, set href to 'events.html'.
  - If related_tool_key corresponds to the carbon calculator, set href to 'carbon_calculator.html'.
  - For other tools, use the application-defined mapping from related_tool_key to URL.
This logic uses no static FAQ content; it only interprets keys.
onRelatedToolLinkClick: Emits an event (entryId, related_tool_key) to faq-entry-list, which then performs navigation with window.location or an app router.

onContactFallbackClick: If the entry itself offers a specific 'contact us about this topic' action (for entries that require human support), triggers navigation to contact.html, optionally with query parameters such as from=faq and faq_id={id} so the contact page can reference this FAQ.

components.functionality: Provides a consistent call-to-action for users who still need help after reading the FAQs, linking directly to contact.html.
data_binding: Controller state.category (for optional inclusion in navigation parameters)
Controller state.q (for optional inclusion in navigation parameters)
event_handlers: onClick: Navigates the browser to contact.html. Optionally appends query parameters such as from=faq and may include category and q for context (e.g., contact.html?from=faq&category={category}&q={encodedSearch}).

components.functionality: Shows a message and optional actions when no FAQ entries match the current category and search filters.
event_handlers: onAdjustFiltersClick: Optionally calls faq-page-controller.onCategoryChange with a neutral/default category and faq-page-controller.onSearchClear to quickly reset filters.
onContactClick: Equivalent to faq-contact-cta.onClick; navigates to contact.html so the user can ask their question directly.

components.functionality: Abstracts reading and updating URL parameters (category, q, entryId) so that all components rely on a single source of truth for URL state. Ensures that user interactions update the URL without full page reloads and that back/forward browser navigation restores the correct FAQ filters and search.
event_handlers: onInit: Parses initial URL query string and supplies category, q, and entryId to the faq-page-controller.

onControllerStateChange: When the controller requests a URL update (e.g., due to category or q changes), constructs a new query string preserving unrelated parameters and calls history.pushState or history.replaceState.

onPopState: Listens for browser back/forward events; re-parses the URL and notifies the faq-page-controller so that the category, q, and entryId in state are updated to match the URL, then triggers a re-filter of faq_entries.

layout.grid_system: Likely a centered, max-width layout based on a 12-column grid with wide outer gutters (desktop breakpoint). Content appears within a central column spanning roughly 8–10 grid columns, with generous side margins.
layout.layout_pattern: The FAQ page keeps the mockup’s linear, editorial flow: a full-width but centered main content band with a pale, nature-tinted background hosts the page title, short introductory copy, and a prominent search bar (this plays the role of the hero text block). Directly beneath this hero-like panel, the FAQ content begins as a wide but centered section. Within that section, the left side is reserved for a slim vertical category filter column, while the right side is a broader question-and-answer area with an accordion list or an empty state. A subtle, full-width contact CTA appears at the bottom of the main content column as the final element in the scroll, mirroring the mockup’s “text then visual” sequence by ending with a clear action block.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The FAQ page keeps the mockup’s linear, editorial flow: a full-width but centered main content band with a pale, nature-tinted background hosts the page title, short introductory copy, and a prominent search bar (this plays the role of the hero text block). Directly beneath this hero-like panel, the FAQ content begins as a wide but centered section. Within that section, the left side is reserved for a slim vertical category filter column, while the right side is a broader question-and-answer area with an accordion list or an empty state. A subtle, full-width contact CTA appears at the bottom of the main content column as the final element in the scroll, mirroring the mockup’s “text then visual” sequence by ending with a clear action block.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The central content column spans roughly 8–10 of 12 grid columns, with generous left and right gutters occupying about 10–12% of the viewport each. The top hero panel (title + intro + search) occupies around the upper 40–50% of the first viewport on desktop thanks to large top and bottom padding (48–64px). Below, the FAQ content section (filters + list) occupies the remaining vertical space and extends beyond the fold as needed. Within this section, the filter sidebar takes about 25–30% of the content width, while the FAQ list wrapper takes about 70–75%. Vertical spacing between major blocks (hero to FAQ section, FAQ list to contact CTA) uses 48–64px margins, and inner spacing between related elements (headings and paragraphs, questions and answers) uses 16–32px, following the 8px spacing scale.
layout_strategies.content_density: The content is deliberately low-density and editorial. Lines of text are relatively short due to the narrow central column and comfortable font sizing, with generous line height. The search bar is large and breathable, not cramped among other controls. Filter options and accordion items have ample padding (around 16–24px internally) and clear separation between them. Even with many FAQ entries, the vertical rhythm remains spacious, avoiding tight stacking or overly compressed lists. The layout prioritizes clarity and calm, even when displaying a longer list of questions.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow preserves the mockup’s F-pattern: the user’s eye enters at the global navigation and logo above, then drops into the large FAQ heading at the left of the hero panel, flows down into the short introductory text, and then to the search bar, which is the main interactive focal point. From there, the eye continues down into the FAQ section where it first touches the filter labels on the left and then sweeps across to the question list on the right. It then progresses vertically through the accordion items and resolves at the bottom with the contact CTA. A floating accessibility button at the bottom-right of the viewport remains a secondary focal point that is visually present but does not interfere with the primary reading and interaction flow.

layout.overall_description: On desktop, the FAQ page feels like a calm, editorial article about finding answers, framed inside a wide, centered column with generous pale background breathing room. At the very top, the site’s existing navigation bar and logo sit quietly across the full width. Below this, a large, soft, nature-tinted hero panel stretches edge-to-edge, yet its content is confined to a central column. At the top-left of this column, a prominent 'FAQ' heading (or similar) appears, followed by a short, multi-line introductory paragraph explaining how to use the help tools. Beneath this text, centered within the same column, a wide search form spans about two-thirds of the content width, with ample whitespace above and below. As you scroll slightly, the hero panel gives way to the main FAQ section: within the same central width, the content splits subtly into two vertical zones—on the left, a slender filter sidebar listing FAQ categories; on the right, a broad, single column of questions in accordion form. Each question block is well spaced, with generous padding and soft dividers that preserve the calm aesthetic. When no results are available, this right-hand zone transforms into a centered empty-state message, possibly with a soft illustration, surrounded by plenty of negative space. At the bottom of the FAQ list, a full-width but low-profile contact CTA card spans the central column, offering a next step if users still need help. Throughout, the rightmost bottom corner remains visually clear except for the small floating accessibility button, which sits above the content without overlapping its key interactive elements.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ page controller acts as the structural frame for the entire FAQ experience. It spans the full browser width but constrains all main content to a centered max-width column aligned to a 12-column grid. At the top, directly beneath the global navigation, it introduces a full-width hero band with a pale, soft green background that extends from the left to the right edges of the viewport. Inside this band, the content is horizontally centered and limited to approximately 8–10 grid columns, leaving wide gutters on both sides. Generous vertical padding (around 64px at the top and 64px at the bottom) ensures that the heading, intro paragraph, and search form feel comfortably spaced. Below this hero band, the page controller transitions into the main FAQ content section on a very light, near-neutral background. Here, the same central column alignment is preserved. Within this section, it lays out a two-area arrangement: a narrow column on the left for the category filter and a wider area on the right for the FAQ list wrapper, both starting at the same vertical position and extending downward as the content grows. At the bottom of the page controller’s flow, after the FAQ list area, it reserves a full-width slot (within the central column) for the contact CTA, with about 48–64px of vertical margin separating it from the preceding FAQ content. Overall, the page controller ensures all components maintain the mockup’s generous side gutters, restrained central width, and clean top-to-bottom reading order.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static structural container; controls max-width, central alignment, section backgrounds, and vertical spacing between hero, FAQ content, and the closing contact CTA. It grows vertically with content and does not scroll independently from the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ search form is positioned within the hero-like top panel, directly beneath the page heading and introductory text. It is horizontally centered within the main content column while still respecting a left alignment of the input’s label and placeholder text. On desktop, the search field and its accompanying button (if present) together span roughly 60–70% of the central column width, leaving comfortable margins on both left and right. There is a vertical gap of about 24–32px between the last line of the intro paragraph and the top edge of the search form, and an additional 32–48px of breathing room below the form before the hero panel transitions into the FAQ content section. The search field itself is tall enough to feel substantial, with internal padding around 12–16px on all sides, making it visually prominent without feeling heavy. The entire search form sits roughly in the lower half of the hero band’s height, contributing to the sense that this top area, like the mockup’s introductory section, is mostly open space framing the heading and search interaction.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element anchored within the hero panel. It does not stick or float; as the user scrolls, it moves off-screen with the rest of the hero content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The category filter sidebar lives in the main FAQ content section, starting just below the hero panel. It aligns flush with the left edge of the central content column and runs vertically down the page. On desktop, it occupies approximately 25–30% of the width of this central column (equivalent to about 3–4 of 12 grid columns), leaving the remaining width to the FAQ list wrapper on the right. The top of the filter sidebar vertically aligns with the top of the FAQ list area, so that the first category label on the left sits next to the first question on the right. Internally, the filter has comfortable padding (around 24px on all sides), and each filter group or category chip is separated by 8–16px vertically, keeping the content light and scannable. The filter’s bottom edge typically does not reach the full height of a long FAQ list; instead, it forms a shorter column of options with ample empty space below, reinforcing the low-density aesthetic. On narrower screens, this sidebar collapses into a full-width block positioned above the FAQ list wrapper, maintaining the same generous padding but spanning close to 100% of the central column width and stacking vertically rather than side-by-side.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On desktop, the filter sidebar is lightly sticky: once scrolled into view, its top edge remains anchored a short distance below the top of the viewport so that category controls stay visible while the FAQ list scrolls alongside it. On mobile or narrow viewports, it becomes a standard static block above the FAQ list without sticky behavior to avoid crowding the screen.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ list wrapper occupies the right-hand portion of the main FAQ content section, starting at the same vertical level as the top of the category filter and extending downward as needed. Horizontally, it begins just to the right of the filter sidebar, separated by a consistent horizontal gap of about 24–32px, and spans the rest of the central column (roughly 70–75% of its width, or about 8–9 grid columns). The wrapper establishes a clear interior vertical rhythm: if a contextual heading or result count is present, it appears at the top of this area, left-aligned with the accordion list beneath and separated from the first question item by about 16–24px. The wrapper maintains a quiet, light background that matches or slightly contrasts with the overall page background, with the accordion items themselves providing the visual structure. There is about 48–64px of vertical space between the bottom of the hero panel and the top of this wrapper, preserving the sense of a distinct section. At the bottom, before the contact CTA, the wrapper ends with around 48px of spacing to ensure the CTA feels like a separate but related follow-up section rather than just another list item.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static container that defines the main vertical scroll area for FAQ entries. It expands vertically to fit the accordion list or empty state content and scrolls with the page. It does not fix or float relative to the viewport.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ entry list is a vertically stacked set of accordion items nested within the FAQ list wrapper. It aligns left with the wrapper’s content edge and spans nearly 100% of the wrapper’s width, leaving only minimal inner horizontal padding (about 16–24px) for breathing room. Each accordion item is separated from the next by a gap of around 16–24px, ensuring that even a longer list feels organized and not cramped. The top of the list begins just below any optional heading or result count, with approximately 16–24px of spacing, so the eye flows naturally from the heading down into the first question. The list can extend well below the fold, but because each item has ample vertical padding, the overall impression remains calm and structured. On mobile, the list continues to be a single column occupying the full available width within the central column, retaining the same vertical spacing between items.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: A standard, scrollable vertical list that expands as more items are present. It has no independent scrolling; it scrolls with the page. When the data state changes (e.g., filters or search), the list’s contents update within the same reserved space without shifting its overall position relative to other components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each FAQ entry item is a full-width accordion card within the FAQ entry list. It stretches from the left to the right inner edge of the list, with consistent padding of about 16–24px on all sides to create a generous hit area for the question and a comfortable reading area for the answer. The collapsed state shows only the question row, which is left-aligned and may include a subtle icon on the right, but visually it remains restrained to match the editorial style. When expanded, the answer content reveals itself directly beneath the question within the same card, indented slightly or aligned with the question text, separated by a small vertical gap of 8–12px. The answer text uses a comfortable line height and avoids full-width lines by respecting the central column limits, resulting in short, easy-to-scan lines similar to the mockup’s paragraph styling. A soft dividing line or light background difference marks the boundaries between items, maintaining visual separation without harsh borders. Vertical spacing between items (16–24px) ensures that even multiple expanded answers do not feel stacked or crowded.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block elements within the list. They expand and contract vertically when toggled but do not affect overall page layout beyond pushing adjacent items up or down. They do not float or stick; their position is defined purely by their order in the list.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ contact CTA is placed at the very bottom of the main FAQ content area, directly below the FAQ list wrapper. It occupies the full width of the central content column, maintaining the same left and right alignment as the hero text and FAQ list. There is a generous vertical gap of about 48–64px between the bottom of the last FAQ item (or the empty state) and the top of this CTA card, visually signaling a new section. The CTA itself is a horizontally oriented block with around 24–32px of internal padding on all sides. The main message—such as 'Still need help?'—is left-aligned at the top-left of the card, and any action button (e.g., 'Contact us') is positioned to the right of the text on larger screens, or below it on smaller screens, while still preserving abundant whitespace. The card’s background can subtly echo the pale green of the hero panel, visually tying the start and end of the page together without overpowering the content. The CTA extends almost to the very bottom of the page content, but a small bottom margin (around 48px) leaves room for the floating accessibility button in the bottom-right to sit clearly above the page edge without overlapping the CTA’s primary action.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block at the end of the document flow. It does not stick or float; it appears naturally as the user scrolls to the bottom of the FAQ list. On very short pages or large displays, it may appear within the first viewport but still remains in a fixed position relative to the FAQ list.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ empty state display occupies the same visual space as the FAQ entry list within the FAQ list wrapper when no results are found. It is centered both horizontally and, when possible, vertically within the wrapper’s visible area to create a calm, focused message. The content—typically an illustration or icon, a short headline, explanatory text, and possibly a button to clear filters—sits in a column that spans about 50–60% of the wrapper’s width, leaving generous side margins. There is ample vertical spacing between these elements: around 16px between the illustration and headline, and 8–16px between headline, body copy, and any action controls. Above and below the empty state block, extra whitespace is maintained (32–48px) to prevent it from feeling cramped within the surrounding layout. The left edge of the text aligns with the left edge of the wrapper’s content column so that it still feels integrated with the page flow, even while being visually centered and slightly more illustrative, echoing the mockup’s use of a large image area beneath the introductory text.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally rendered static content inside the FAQ list wrapper. It replaces the accordion list when no items match the current search or filters, without shifting the wrapper’s position on the page. It scrolls with the page like any other block content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The URL state manager has no direct visual representation on the page; it functions as an invisible behavioral layer connecting the search form, filter sidebar, and FAQ list wrapper. Conceptually, it sits alongside these components in the page hierarchy, ensuring that changes to search queries, selected categories, or opened accordion items are reflected in the browser’s URL parameters. This allows users to share or revisit a specific FAQ state without altering the layout or introducing extra UI elements. From a spatial perspective, it does not consume any width or height and does not appear as a visible block; its presence is only felt in how the visible components update and maintain state.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, logic-only component with no impact on layout geometry. It operates behind the scenes, updating URL state and responding to URL changes to adjust the content within the existing layout without causing reflow beyond content updates inside the FAQ list wrapper.

name: Privacy Policy
title: Privacy Policy
description: Page that retrieves and displays the site's privacy policy content, with section navigation, in-page search, and a direct link to contact the team about privacy questions.

core_features: Load the latest privacy policy content (last updated date and all sections) via getPrivacyPolicyContent when the page is opened or refreshed.
Display the privacy policy as a list of sections, each with a title and body, using the section_id as an internal anchor.
Show the policy's 'last updated' information based on the returned last_updated value.
Provide an in-page table of contents that links to specific policy sections and updates URL parameters for deep-linking.
Support deep-linking to a specific section using a URL parameter (e.g., privacy.html?section=<section_id>) so external links or internal navigation can open the page scrolled to that section.
Provide a simple in-page text search that filters and/or highlights sections based on a URL parameter (e.g., privacy.html?q=<search-term>) and a search form.
Offer a clear navigation link to contact.html specifically for privacy-related questions (e.g., 'Contact us about privacy'), optionally passing a topic URL parameter.
Handle URL parameter changes (section, q) without using localStorage, keeping the page state in sync with the current URL.
user_workflows:
Read the privacy policy from start to finish:
1. User navigates to privacy.html (e.g., via footer link).
2. Page initialization component calls getPrivacyPolicyContent.
3. Once data is returned, the header component displays the policy title and last_updated date.
4. The section list component renders all sections in the order received from the API.
5. The user scrolls to read all sections; no additional user input is required.

Jump directly to a specific privacy policy section:
1. User clicks a section name in the table of contents OR opens a direct link like privacy.html?section=<section_id>.
2. On initial load, the page reads the 'section' URL parameter (if present) and sets the selectedSectionId state.
3. The section list component scrolls to or visually emphasizes the section whose section_id matches selectedSectionId.
4. If the user clicks a different section in the table of contents, the selectedSectionId state updates and the URL parameter 'section' is updated accordingly (using history APIs or a full navigation), keeping deep links consistent.

Search within the privacy policy:
1. User navigates to privacy.html or arrives with a URL like privacy.html?q=<search-term>.
2. Page initialization calls getPrivacyPolicyContent.
3. The search component reads the 'q' URL parameter (if present) and sets the searchQuery state.
4. The search component runs a client-side search over the loaded sections (titles and bodies) using searchQuery.
5. The section list component displays all sections but can either filter to matching sections or highlight matching text, depending on the product decision.
6. If the user types into the search input and submits the form, the searchQuery state is updated and the URL parameter 'q' is updated to match.
7. The results immediately update to reflect the new query.

Contact the team with privacy questions from the policy page:
1. User is reading the privacy policy sections.
2. At one or more appropriate spots in the policy content area, a dedicated 'Contact us about privacy' link is displayed.
3. When the user clicks this link, the browser navigates to contact.html with a URL parameter indicating the topic is privacy (e.g., contact.html?topic=privacy), without storing any state in localStorage.
4. The contact page can then pre-fill or adjust its form based on the topic parameter (handled on that page).

Follow an external link to a specific privacy topic:
1. An external site or internal consent dialog links to privacy.html with optional parameters, such as privacy.html?section=<section_id>&q=<search-term>.
2. The privacy policy page loads and calls getPrivacyPolicyContent.
3. Once content is available, the page reads both 'section' and 'q' URL parameters.
4. The section list component scrolls to the specified section_id while the search component applies the search term, so the user sees the relevant portions of the policy immediately.
5. The user can clear or change the search query using the search form, which updates the 'q' URL parameter accordingly.
interactions: On page load: Trigger getPrivacyPolicyContent to fetch the latest privacy policy data. When the promise resolves, update internal state with last_updated and sections, then render all dependent components.

Click on table of contents entry: User clicks a section title in the table of contents; the handler prevents default behavior if necessary, updates the 'section' URL parameter to the corresponding section_id, updates selectedSectionId state, and scrolls to the anchor for that section.

Direct URL navigation with section parameter: If the user navigates to privacy.html with ?section=<section_id>, the initialization logic reads this parameter and sets selectedSectionId so that the corresponding section is scrolled into view after content is loaded.

Direct URL navigation with search parameter: If the user navigates to privacy.html with ?q=<search-term>, the initialization logic reads this parameter, sets searchQuery state, and triggers client-side search highlighting or filtering over the policy sections once they are loaded.

Submit in-page search form: User enters text into the search input and submits the form; the handler prevents default form submission, updates searchQuery state, updates the 'q' URL parameter to match the entered value, and recalculates search results (e.g., filtered list or highlighting) over the loaded sections.

Clear search query: User clears the search input and submits the empty form or clicks a dedicated 'clear' control; the handler sets searchQuery to an empty value, removes the 'q' parameter from the URL (or sets it to an empty string), and restores the unfiltered view of all sections.

Click 'Contact us about privacy' link: User clicks this link in the policy content area; the browser navigates to contact.html, optionally passing a topic parameter (e.g., contact.html?topic=privacy) to indicate the context of the inquiry.

Hash/anchor navigation: When the user clicks a section link that uses a fragment identifier (e.g., #<section_id>), the browser scrolls to that section's anchor. Optional logic can listen for hash changes to keep selectedSectionId state synchronized with the fragment.

Browser back/forward navigation: When the user uses the browser's back/forward buttons after interacting with the search or table of contents, the page reads the updated URL parameters (section, q) in a popstate or similar handler and updates selectedSectionId and searchQuery states so displayed content remains consistent with the URL.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The page relies exclusively on URL parameters and in-memory state (no localStorage) to control which parts of the policy are emphasized or filtered.

- URL Parameters:
  - section: When present, section=<section_id> indicates which policy section should be considered 'active' or scrolled into view after getPrivacyPolicyContent data is loaded. This value must match a section_id returned from the API for a visible effect.
  - q: When present, q=<search-term> represents the current in-page search query. The search component uses this value to perform a client-side search over the loaded sections.

- Initial Load:
  1. On first render of privacy.html, the page parses the current URL search parameters and stores them in local state variables, e.g., selectedSectionId and searchQuery.
  2. The page calls getPrivacyPolicyContent and stores the response in policyContent state (including last_updated and sections).
  3. Once sections are available, components that depend on selectedSectionId and searchQuery initialize their behavior (scrolling to the active section, filtering/highlighting search results).

- State Updates and URL Synchronization:
  - When the user selects a section from the table of contents, the selectedSectionId state is updated and the 'section' parameter in the URL is updated to that section_id. This can be done via history.pushState or by navigating to the updated URL, ensuring the link is shareable.
  - When the user enters or changes a search query and submits the search form, searchQuery state is updated and the 'q' parameter in the URL is set or updated. Clearing the search removes or empties the 'q' parameter.
  - Internal state is always derived from the URL on initial page load and on browser navigation events, so reloading or sharing the URL preserves the user's view.

- No Persistent Client Storage:
  - The page does not use localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies to store UI state for this privacy policy view. All state restoration is driven by the URL parameters and the dynamic content returned by getPrivacyPolicyContent.

components.functionality: On page load, call getPrivacyPolicyContent to retrieve the privacy policy data, parse URL parameters (section, q), and initialize in-memory state used by all other components on this page.
Display the page title 'Privacy Policy' along with the dynamically fetched last_updated date from the privacy policy content.

Render a table of contents listing all sections returned by getPrivacyPolicyContent. Each entry links to the corresponding section using its section_id and updates the URL 'section' parameter and selectedSectionId state.

Provide an input for users to search within the privacy policy content. On submit, update searchQuery state and the 'q' URL parameter; trigger client-side filtering or highlighting in the section list based on the current query.

Render all privacy policy sections (title and body) in the order provided by getPrivacyPolicyContent. Each section is wrapped in an element with an id based on section_id to support anchor navigation. Uses selectedSectionId to scroll to or visually highlight the active section and searchQuery to filter or highlight matching text.
Render a dedicated link for users to contact the organization with privacy-related questions. Navigates to contact.html and may append a topic URL parameter for context (e.g., topic=privacy).

Keep internal state (selectedSectionId and searchQuery) synchronized with the URL parameters (section and q), both on initial load and when the browser history changes (back/forward). When components update these states, this component also updates the URL parameters without reloading the page.

layout.grid_system: Likely a centered, max-width layout based on a 12-column grid with wide outer gutters (desktop breakpoint). Content appears within a central column spanning roughly 8–10 grid columns, with generous side margins.
layout.layout_pattern: The page uses a single, centered main column within a full-width layout. At the top of the main content, a pale green hero-style panel introduces the Privacy Policy with a prominent heading and short explanatory text, analogous to the 'Biodiversity' hero from the mockup. Directly beneath this intro, still within the same central column, are the functional tools: first the in-page search, then the table of contents as a vertical list of section links. Below this hero area, the detailed policy sections occupy a long, scrollable white canvas in the same central column. A small, clearly separated contact link block appears at the bottom of the policy content. Invisible utility components (data loader, URL state sync) sit behind the scenes and do not affect the visual layout.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page uses a single, centered main column within a full-width layout. At the top of the main content, a pale green hero-style panel introduces the Privacy Policy with a prominent heading and short explanatory text, analogous to the 'Biodiversity' hero from the mockup. Directly beneath this intro, still within the same central column, are the functional tools: first the in-page search, then the table of contents as a vertical list of section links. Below this hero area, the detailed policy sections occupy a long, scrollable white canvas in the same central column. A small, clearly separated contact link block appears at the bottom of the policy content. Invisible utility components (data loader, URL state sync) sit behind the scenes and do not affect the visual layout.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The main column spans roughly 70–80% of the page width on desktop (about 8–10 columns of a 12-column grid), with 10–15% gutters on either side. The hero/privacy-intro area plus search and TOC together occupy about the top 50–60% of the initial viewport height on desktop, with generous vertical padding (32–48px top and bottom) inside the pale green panel. The policy sections below extend vertically as needed and are separated from the hero by a clear 48–64px gap. The contact link block at the bottom has its own 32–48px vertical padding to feel intentional rather than an afterthought.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density remains low and editorial. Text lines are relatively short due to the constrained central column and generous line height; paragraphs have comfortable 16–24px spacing between them. The search field and TOC are given generous breathing room, with 24–32px gaps around them, avoiding a tool-heavy, crowded feeling. The policy sections themselves are visually broken into clearly separated blocks with 32–48px spacing between sections and 16–24px between headings and paragraphs. Overall, the layout favors calm readability over packing in as much text as possible per screen.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow maintains the mockup’s strong vertical F-pattern. Eyes enter at the top left with the site header (outside this spec), then drop into the large 'Privacy Policy' heading at the top-left of the pale green hero panel. From there, the gaze moves down to the brief descriptive text, then continues down to the search bar as the primary functional control. Below that, the TOC provides a quick scan of section titles, guiding the user’s path into the detailed content. As users scroll, their focus shifts down through the stacked policy sections, concluding with the contact link block as a clear next step for questions. Any global floating elements (like an accessibility button) remain secondary and do not intersect with this main vertical reading flow.

layout.overall_description: The Privacy Policy page appears as a calm, editorial document set within a centered, pale green hero area that transitions into a spacious white reading surface. After the global navigation, the main content begins with a wide but contained panel in a very soft green, spanning about three-quarters of the page width and centered horizontally. Within this panel, the 'Privacy Policy' heading is left-aligned and large, with ample space above and below, followed by a short explanatory paragraph and metadata like the last updated date, all with generous line spacing. Beneath this intro, still within the same column, the in-page search field sits as a clear, single-line control, followed by a vertical table of contents listing each policy section. This top block—intro text, search, and TOC—collectively fills about the upper half of the initial viewport on desktop, giving a strong sense of orientation without feeling cramped. Below, the green background gives way to a white background where the actual policy sections are presented one after another, each section heading left-aligned with comfortable spacing and body text that is easy to scan and read. After the final section, an isolated, slightly emphasized contact link block invites users to reach out with privacy questions, separated by a generous vertical gap so it reads as a clear closing element. Throughout, the central column and large side gutters echo the original mockup’s airy, nature-inspired editorial style, with no visual clutter or heavy chrome around the tools.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This data-loader component operates entirely behind the scenes and does not occupy any visible space in the layout. Conceptually, it sits at the very top of the main content hierarchy, ensuring that policy data is loaded before the visible header, search, TOC, and sections render. It does not introduce any padding, margins, or visual blocks, and thus does not disrupt the vertical rhythm of the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, zero-size element that runs before or alongside initial render; it has no spatial footprint and does not influence surrounding layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This state-sync component also has no visible representation in the UI. It conceptually sits at the same hierarchy level as the data loader, wiring the URL hash or query parameters to the active policy section and search state. It is not rendered as any box, control, or text and therefore does not alter spacing, alignment, or flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, zero-size element that synchronizes scroll position and selected section with the URL; it remains outside the visual flow and does not affect layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Positioned at the top of the main content area, this meta-info header forms the upper portion of a large, pale green hero panel that echoes the 'Biodiversity' hero from the mockup. On desktop, the hero panel spans roughly 70–80% of the viewport width, centered horizontally, with wide gutters on both sides. Inside this panel, the 'Privacy Policy' H1 is aligned to the left edge of the central content column, starting approximately 64px below the bottom edge of the global navigation. The heading occupies one to two lines with generous line height, visually dominating the upper third of the hero area. Below the heading, separated by about 16–24px, sits a short explanatory paragraph and any meta details (such as 'Last updated' and overview text). These lines are also left-aligned with a comfortable 16–24px spacing between them, maintaining a light editorial feel. The entire header block (heading plus meta text) is vertically padded with roughly 32–48px above and below so the text appears to float in space rather than press against the panel edges. On smaller viewports, the hero panel narrows with reduced side gutters, but the left-aligned column structure and generous vertical breathing room are preserved.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block element at the top of the main content, forming part of a full-width but centrally constrained hero panel; it scrolls away naturally as the user moves down the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The in-page search form sits directly beneath the header text within the same central column and pale green hero panel, reading as the primary interactive tool tied to the policy. It is separated from the meta-info paragraph above by approximately 24–32px of vertical space. The search box itself is horizontally centered within the main content column but left-aligned text-wise, spanning about 60–70% of the column width on desktop to maintain a calm, substantial presence without appearing oversized. There is ample white (or very light green) space above and below the field, with roughly 16px of padding within the input and a total of about 24px of vertical breathing room before the next component (the TOC). Any label or helper text is placed directly above the field, left-aligned, but with a small 8–12px gap so the field remains visually dominant. The search area visually occupies a low, horizontal band across the hero panel, acting as a subtle but clear focal point in the middle of the initial viewport.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level element within the hero panel, part of the vertical flow. On small screens it expands to nearly full width of the content column while preserving generous vertical margins.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The table of contents (section navigation list) is placed immediately below the search form, still within the pale green hero panel and the same central column. It begins about 24–32px below the bottom of the search field, maintaining the relaxed vertical rhythm. The TOC presents as a simple, left-aligned vertical list of section titles, each item occupying its own line with 8–12px spacing between items. The entire TOC block spans roughly 70–80% of the content column width, starting flush with the left text edge used by the header and body copy, and leaving comfortable empty space on the right. The block is framed by approximately 24–32px of padding above and below so it feels like a distinct navigation zone but still integrated into the hero area. On desktop, the combined header + search + TOC content fills about the top half of the viewport, so the TOC is often visible at the bottom of the initial fold, inviting users to jump into sections. On smaller screens, the TOC remains stacked below the search and may extend beyond the initial viewport, but always as part of this top block before the detailed policy content begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block element that participates in normal scrolling. Optionally, the TOC header row (e.g., 'On this page') could be made sticky within the viewport once scrolled, but the main TOC list itself remains within the vertical document flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The section list forms the main body of the page and begins just below the hero-style panel. There is a clear transition: approximately 48–64px of vertical gap separates the bottom of the pale green hero (TOC) from the start of the first policy section, and the background shifts to pure white (or the site’s base background color). Each policy section is rendered as a left-aligned block within the same central column width as the header, preserving line length and side gutters. Section headings (e.g., H2s) sit at the top of each block, with 24–32px of vertical space above them and 16–24px below before the body text begins. Paragraphs within a section are spaced at around 16–20px apart, with a comfortable line height that mirrors the editorial tone from the mockup. Sections are separated from each other by a generous 32–48px gap so that each feels distinct and scannable when scrolling. On desktop, this list extends vertically, becoming the primary reading surface; the user’s scroll path goes linearly from the first section at the top to the last section near the bottom. The central alignment, ample whitespace, and relatively short line length maintain the airy, research-oriented feel even though the content is text-heavy.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrollable content area forming the majority of the page length. Sections may be targeted by anchor links from the TOC, causing the viewport to scroll smoothly to the relevant heading, but the block itself is part of the normal document flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact link appears as a distinct closing block at the bottom of the policy section list, giving users a clear next action if they have privacy questions. It is separated from the final policy section by a generous 48–64px vertical gap so it reads as a concluding element rather than just another paragraph. Within the central column, the block may include a short, left-aligned sentence (such as 'Have questions about this policy?') with the contact link styled more prominently (e.g., accent color) inline or on the next line. This block spans roughly the same width as the text above, maintains the same left alignment, and includes about 24px of padding above and below to create a subtle band of emphasis without using heavy visual treatments. There is additional 64px of bottom margin before the end of the page or footer, leaving breathing room below the link and maintaining the minimalist aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level element at the bottom of the main content flow; it scrolls into view after the user reaches the end of the policy sections and does not stick or float.
